latest.content:
Today 17:13
RNS Number : 0047V
Atlas Metals Group PLC
02 March 2026
2 March 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Total Voting Rights
Atlas Metals (LSE: AMG), the natural resources and energy company
,
in conformity with the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rule 5.6.1R, announces that, as at 27 February 2026, its issued share capital comprised 30,532,930
ordinary shares of nominal value £0.01 each ("
Ordinary Shares
"). Each Ordinary Share carries the right to one vote in relation to all circumstances at general meetings of the Company. The Company does not hold any shares in treasury and therefore the total number of voting rights attributable to the Ordinary Shares is 30,532,930.
The above figure of 30,532,930 may be used by shareholders of the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
- Ends -
For further information, please contact:
Atlas Metals Group plc:

latest.title: Issue of Equity
latest.content:
Today 15:18
RNS Number : 7222T
Atlas Metals Group PLC
19 February 2026
19 February 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Issue of equity
Atlas Metals (LON: AMG), the natural resources and energy company announces that, further to the Company's circular dated 5 November 2025 and the authorities approved by shareholders of the Company at the general meeting held on 1 December 2025, application has been made to London Stock Exchange plc (the "
London Stock Exchange
") for the admission of 1,408,000 ordinary shares of nominal value £0.01 each ("
Ordinary Shares
") to be admitted to trading on the main market for listed securities of the London Stock Exchange ("
Admission
").
Admission relates to the allotment of 1,408,000 new Ordinary Shares ("
New Shares
") to be issued in terms of a historic settlement agreement.
Admission of the New Shares is expected to occur on 20 February 2026. The New Shares will rank
pari passu
in all respects with the existing Ordinary Shares.
Following Admission, the Company's issued ordinary share capital will comprise 30,532,930 Ordinary Shares, each carrying one vote. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. The total number of voting rights in the Company following Admission will therefore be 30,532,930. With effect from Admission, this figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

latest.title: Update on Proposed Acquisition
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 0418T
Atlas Metals Group PLC
16 February 2026
16 February 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Update on Proposed Acquisition
Atlas Metals (LON: AMG), the natural resources and energy company is pleased to provide an update
on the Company's proposed acquisition of Universal Pozzolanic Silica Alumina Ltd ("
UPSA
") (the "
Proposed Acquisition
").
As detailed in the Company's announcement on 22 December 2025, the Company engaged SLR Consulting Australia Pty Ltd ("
SLR
"), to conduct a confirmatory drilling campaign, commencing on Lots 7 and 8 at the Yammacoona Sand Quarry in New South Wales, Australia, with the intention of proving a measured resource, and a technical memorandum has been provided by SLR. A total of 26 drillholes have now been completed using three drilling methods, diamond, percussion and blast hole, for a total of 1,265 metres.
From the samples collected during the drilling programme Quantitative X-Ray Diffraction (QXRD) and clay mineralogical analyses will be undertaken to characterise material properties relevant to assessing pozzolanic potential. Drill core pieces representing all lithological units were also selected for petrographic examination and X-ray Fluorescence (XRF) analysis to document mineralogical composition and textural features.
Initial volumes pending validation of drilling data, interpretation, geological modelling and material test results within Lots 7 and 8 indicate that the concept pit (see figure 1 below) covered by the areas of Lots 7 and 8 contains a total of
86.5 million tonnes of pozzolanic sandstone from a total volume of 151.75 million tonnes of material. Whilst these initial volumes are being assessed for resource status pending material testing and validation, the purpose of the update is to increase confidence in the volumes towards measured status. Volumes may vary based on further development of the geological model and validation.
Atlas Metals is expecting SLR to complete the updated Competent Person's Report ("
CPR
"), using data obtained from this drilling programme, which is expected to include certain JORC resources being upgraded from the inferred to measured category, prior to the completion of the Proposed Acquisition. The last published CPR on the Yammacoona Sand Quarry, as announced by the Company on 28 November 2025, may be found on the Company's website at:
https://atlasmetalsgroup.com/investors/reports
The Proposed Acquisition continues to progress in line with the Board's expectations and further announcements on the progress of the Proposed Acquisition will be made when appropriate.
Figure 1 - Concept Pit Design, showing the locations of blocks 7 and 8 (from SLR CPR 26 November 2025
Illustration: Recent SLR drilling on behalf of the Company at the Yammacoona Sand Quarry in New South Wales, Australia

company_30s.what_they_do: AMG.L is a small uranium exploration and mining group that focuses on developing a uranium asset in Kyrgyzstan. They conduct geological surveys, drilling, and preliminary development work to bring the asset into a production‑ready state.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Uranium is a critical component of the growing nuclear power sector, especially as governments seek low‑carbon energy sources. AMG’s project sits in a region with proven uranium resources and could become a supplier for the global nuclear market if the project reaches commercial scale.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in a capital‑raising phase, with several equity issues and an ATM facility in place to manage cash flow. Recent RNS filings show a mix of corporate governance updates (voting rights, director dealings) and asset progress reports. The share price is trading at a very low level, reflecting the uncertainty around the project’s timeline and funding.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy adjusting its capital structure – issuing new shares, updating voting rights, and using an ATM facility to manage liquidity.
At the same time, it’s pushing forward on the Kyrgyz uranium project, with updates on holdings and potential acquisition plans that could bring in strategic partners or additional funding.

These moves suggest a company that is actively trying to shore up its balance sheet while keeping the asset alive, but the lack of a clear, recent operational milestone means the market remains uncertain.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet speculation – no clear consensus, but a handful of bullish voices hint at progress.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders highlight the recent progress updates and the possibility of a strategic acquisition, suggesting that the company might be on the brink of a positive catalyst.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others call it a potential scam or express skepticism about the company’s ability to deliver on its promises, citing the high risk of a small, resource‑heavy play.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has been largely neutral, with a slight uptick in bullish posts after the latest RNS about the acquisition. However, overall engagement remains low, keeping the mood subdued.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – the stock has no recent search interest, which means a fresh catalyst could surprise the market.

near_term_catalysts: A formal announcement of a partnership or acquisition that would inject capital and expertise into the Kyrgyz project.
Completion of a key drilling phase that confirms resource potential, which could be announced in the next RNS.
An increase in share price volume that exceeds 10‑day average, indicating institutional interest following a capital call or new share issue.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in trading volume, a clear statement of project milestones, or a change in the company’s voting rights that signals a new shareholder structure. Any announcement that reduces the risk of capital dilution or confirms a funding round should be a trigger.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a potential recovery cycle – the recent capital‑raising activity suggests that capitulation may be ending, but the company still needs a concrete milestone to move past the uncertainty phase.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the system has not yet signaled AMG.L, and there is no clear evidence of a deep crash. The company is still in a funding and development phase, so any upside is contingent on a catalyst.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company secures a strategic partnership or completes a positive drilling result, the uranium asset could become a valuable source for nuclear utilities. A 50‑70% recovery from the current price would deliver a strong return, especially given the low valuation relative to the asset’s potential.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risks are capital dilution from further share issues, regulatory delays in Kyrgyzstan, and the inherent uncertainty of a small exploration project. A failure to secure funding could push the share price back into the lower range.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is high if a catalyst arrives – a 70% move up would be a 2:1 reward for the risk of a small downside. However, the current lack of a clear signal and the need for a concrete milestone keep the risk side uncertain.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter only after a clear, positive announcement (e.g., partnership, drilling success) and confirm with a volume spike. Position size should be a small portion of the portfolio (5–10%) and set a stop 15% below the entry to protect against a temporary dip.
playbook.if_watching: Track the next RNS and monitor share price for a 10‑day volume breakout. If volume rises and the price moves above the recent 50‑day moving average, consider a partial entry.
playbook.if_passing: There are no imminent terminal risks like delisting or bankruptcy, so the playbook does not include a pass scenario. Focus on waiting for a catalyst.

ai_insights.bottom_line: AMG.L is a uranium exploration firm in a funding phase; a clear partnership or drilling success could trigger a meaningful bounce, but the lack of a current deep‑crash signal keeps the outlook neutral.
